title: Characters Appearances Pre and Post Timeskip split
display_title: Characters Appearances Pre and Post Timeskip split
wikitext:
[]: Characters' Appearances Pre- and Post-Timeskip [split]             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Apr 10, 2014 3:34 am [Split from "Why didn't Gendo age at all during the timeskip?" - Monk] Kendrix wrote: Misato needs to retain some imposingness, but she's smaller now, way less body fat. )

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Apr 10, 2014 9:40 am I like to imagine that years of rigorous discipline and punishing herself with an intensive exercise routine has turned Misato into the ultimate milf under that coat. Every day. Sometimes twice.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Apr 10, 2014 10:57 am 'cept Misato don't got no kids, Chuckles.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Apr 10, 2014 12:10 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: 'cept Misato don't got no kids, Chuckles.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Apr 10, 2014 3:02 pm ElMariachi wrote: How can you say that, she spent the whole movie encased in her heavy Captain coat? For all we know she's still hot and voluptuous under it, or even as bodybuilt as Mikasa Ackerman!

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Apr 10, 2014 4:28 pm Kendrix wrote: We can still pretty much make out a fair bit of her silhouette. Just what we see of her legs and arms. It's also the hairknot, this whole air of 'healthy warmth' is gone.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Apr 10, 2014 4:41 pm She certainly seems more petite, at any rate.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Apr 10, 2014 4:45 pm ^ Well of course, before she was wearing a form fitting top with her opened leather jacket, and now is in a thick turtleneck sweater and heavy Captain coat. And even then you can still see the forms of her chest!

[]:             Quote Post by Yaywalter » Thu Apr 10, 2014 9:17 pm Chuckman wrote: That can be taken care of.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Apr 11, 2014 9:13 am ElMariachi wrote: ^ Well of course, before she was wearing a form fitting top with her opened leather jacket, and now is in a thick turtleneck sweater and heavy Captain coat. And even then you can still see the forms of her chest!

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Apr 11, 2014 9:28 am Well her pre-3I turtleneck top is sleeveless and her red jacket is part of her Nerv uniform. That seems like a reasonable compromise of comfort vs formality.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Fri Apr 11, 2014 9:29 am It's a turtleneck but not a sweater. The base is probably air conditioned. Where it's actually hot she hangs around half naked.

[]:             Quote Post by Dataprime » Fri Apr 11, 2014 9:48 am I just want to know where she got those sweet glasses from

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Apr 11, 2014 10:07 am She's the Captain of WILLE's most powerful fleet, she probably ordered them made specially for her, and I'm sure the guy who did them made them for free just for the great Captain Katsuragi!

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Sun Apr 13, 2014 4:37 pm Misato always seemed to have this extreme back and forth between flaunting her body and being ashamed of it at the same time. The sweaters, heavy coats, turtlenecks could be part of that. Though, to be fair, I think a lot of her clothes were picked to give her a 'cool tough lady' look.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Apr 13, 2014 4:44 pm Alternatively, it could be because she's constantly in an environment of gaseous LCL.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Apr 13, 2014 4:57 pm Or maybe it could be because they are in the freakin' North Pole! Everyone in the Wunder has an heavy turtleneck sweater as part of their uniform, the only exception is Sakura who's a medical officer and thus probably spending most of her time in the nursery where there's heating for the wounded people, and the Eva pilots when they are wearing their plugsuits (and even then Asuka is wearing a turtlenecked track jacket and a cap)

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Apr 14, 2014 5:20 am Everything does seem rather iced when we get that panorama shot of where the Wunder was camping. Misato's change in appearance is sure effective at conveying her change in personality. There's almost no skin left uncovered, and then there's the hair knot.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Apr 14, 2014 6:31 am ^ Oh, there's no doubt that Misato's new attire was meant to take away all the warm she previously had in Jo and Ha, and if it wasn't clear enough, she even sports most of the time big glasses completely hiding her eyes, like Gendo did before. (except that he took it a step even further with his visor!) I was just trying to find an in-universe reason for that attire, and the frozen seas around them seems a good explanation!

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Apr 14, 2014 6:38 am It's pretty much a very classic 'warship captain attire', tho. Theb neat thing about the sunglasses is that she also wore some when she was first introduced, and that this adds another, parallel scene where she takes them off in a "revealing" fashion. 1.11 *sunglasses get removed* "Oh, just go ahead and first name me, I'm the token nice person!" 3.33 *sunglasses get removed* "You will never do anything at all..." Also, look at my glorious death glare and how my eyes are suddenly drawn very differently.
summary:
[]: Characters' Appearances Pre- and Post-Timeskip [split]             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Apr 10, 2014 3:34 am [Split from "Why didn't Gendo age at all during the timeskip?" - Monk] Kendrix wrote: Misato needs to retain some imposingness, but she's smaller now, way less body fat. )

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Apr 10, 2014 9:40 am I li
